definitions: The time constant characterizing the exponential decay of a perturbation from equilibrium in a physical system.
In materials science, the time required for a material to return to its equilibrium state after being subjected to a stress or strain.
In nuclear magnetic resonance, the time taken for the nuclear spins to return to their equilibrium distribution after being perturbed.
examples: The relaxation time of the polymer was measured.
Understanding the relaxation time is crucial for designing new materials.
The long relaxation time of the system made experiments challenging.
